key_strengths: World's largest vertically integrated eyewear company with ~27% global market share and no comparable competitor
Non-discretionary demand driven by rising global myopia and aging demographics provides secular growth for decades
Triple moat: lens technology switching costs, iconic brand portfolio (Ray-Ban, Oakley, 20+ luxury licenses), and 18,000+ owned retail stores
FCF conversion above 140% of net income with tangible ROIC of 15-20% once merger goodwill is stripped
Trading at 22.5x forward P/E versus 5-year average of 30-35x, with 99.9% Monte Carlo probability of undervaluation
key_risks: Delfin succession crisis: founder's 8 heirs unable to agree on governance for 3 years, freezing dividends and creating strategic uncertainty
Smart glasses margin dilution: Ray-Ban Meta drove 280bps gross margin decline in FY2025; unclear if margins improve at scale
Tariff and FX headwinds: ~40% US revenue exposure with EUR reporting creates earnings translation risk
Apple's announced 2027 entry into smart glasses directly challenges Ray-Ban Meta growth narrative
Rising debt (+18% in 3 years) and declining interest coverage (20x to 11x) with ongoing M&A appetite
red_flags: Gross margin fell 280bps in a single year (60.6% vs 63.4%) -- largest annual decline in available history
CEO holds dual Chairman/CEO role with minimal direct equity ownership
Reported ROIC of 5.2% below WACC of 6.7% on GAAP basis due to EUR 30B goodwill
Price sits 10.5% above quant bear IV -- no traditional conservative margin of safety
buy_triggers: Price falls to EUR 175 or below (at or below bear IV), providing traditional margin of safety
Delfin succession resolved with Leonardo Maria gaining effective control, removing governance overhang
Smart glasses margins shown improving toward corporate average in FY2026 results
sell_triggers: Adjusted ROIC (ex-goodwill) falls below 8% for two consecutive years, indicating moat erosion
Stock reaches EUR 380-400 (bull IV territory, >20x forward EV/EBITDA)
Serial value-destroying acquisitions beyond Supreme signaling strategic drift from core optical competence
valuation_summary: Stock trades at EUR 194.75, a 32% discount to base IV of EUR 286 and 40% below November 2025 highs. Forward P/E of 22.5x is near a 5-year low despite record 11.2% organic revenue growth. All 25 DCF sensitivity grid cells produce IV above current price. Monte Carlo 5th percentile of EUR 247 is 27% above current price. The market is pricing in growth collapse and permanent multiple compression that contradicts FY2025 record results.
compact_checklist: EssilorLuxottica sells prescription lenses, branded eyewear frames, and smart glasses through 18,000+ owned stores and wholesale channels, driven by non-discretionary vision correction demand that grows with aging demographics and rising global myopia.

Vertical integration across lenses, frames, and retail at a scale no competitor can replicate, combined with optician switching costs on Essilor lens technology and the world's strongest eyewear brand portfolio (Ray-Ban, Oakley, 20+ luxury licenses), produces 60%+ gross margins and ~27% global market share.

Tangible ROIC is 15-20% once you strip EUR 30B of merger goodwill, FCF conversion runs above 140% of net income, and capex at 5.4% of revenue confirms asset-light compounding -- though gross margin fell 280bps in FY2025 from smart glasses mix dilution.

CEO Milleri has executed well operationally (11.2% constant-currency growth, margin expansion roadmap to 19-20%), but he holds minimal equity, serves as dual Chairman/CEO, and the Delfin controlling shareholder has been paralyzed by a three-year succession dispute among the founder's eight heirs.

Net debt/EBITDA of 1.1x, A2/A credit ratings, EUR 3.5B cash on hand, and EUR 3.8B annual FCF mean EssilorLuxottica would remain FCF-positive even in a 30% revenue decline scenario -- this balance sheet is a competitive weapon, not a liability.

At EUR 195 and a 22.5x forward P/E (versus a 5-year average of 30-35x), the market is pricing in an immediate growth collapse to low-single-digits and permanent multiple compression, which contradicts record 11.2% organic growth and Q4 acceleration to 18.4%.

The business quality moat is fortress-grade and Monte Carlo shows 99.9% probability of undervaluation, but the price sits 10% above the quant bear IV of EUR 176, so a truly conservative buyer would prefer to enter closer to EUR 175 for maximum downside protection.
Sell if adjusted ROIC (ex-goodwill) falls below 8% for two consecutive years, signaling the vertically integrated moat is eroding rather than compounding.
source_summary: EssilorLuxottica FY2025 earnings release (Feb 2026), Yahoo Finance (yfinance auto-fetch), quant DCF model (src/quant), Moody's credit opinion (Sep 2025), S&P Global rating affirmation (Oct 2024), Bloomberg, CNBC, analyst consensus (22 analysts), company investor presentations, GlobeNewsWire press releases
